subcodes.title: Words to Mary Hanford Ford around Oct. 1911
subcodes.first_line: The utterances of Bahá'u'lláh are to be translated into deeds... It is possible to so adjust oneself to the practice of nobility that its atmosphere surrounds us and colors all our acts
subcodes.subjects: @corruption and renewal; abandoning religious dogmas, @walking the spiritual path; spiritual wayfaring; the virtuous life, @unity; oneness of humanity, @service to others; to the Cause of God, @words vs deeds
